title: build_shielded_tx: core Sapling transaction builder
description:
Implement the main function in wallet/src/tx_builder.rs:

pub fn build_shielded_tx(
    sk: &SaplingExtendedSpendingKey,
    to_address: &str,
    amount_zatoshi: u64,
    memo: &[u8; 512],
    notes: &[SpendableNote],        -- from spendable_notes() + witness_data blobs
    params: &(SpendParameters, OutputParameters),
    network: ZcashNetwork,
    anchor_height: u64,
) -> Result<Vec<u8>, TxBuildError>

Algorithm:
1. Validate amount_zatoshi >= DUST_THRESHOLD (const = 1_000 zatoshi; cite ZIP-317 in comment)
2. Parse to_address with zcash_address — extract Sapling PaymentAddress; reject transparent/Orchard-only with TxBuildError::UnsupportedAddressType
3. Compute fee using fees::zip317_fee(sapling_logical_actions(n_spends, n_outputs=1+change_output))
4. Select notes using fees::select_notes(notes, amount_zatoshi, fee) — propagate CoinSelectError to TxBuildError
5. Reconstruct each selected note via spendable_note_to_sapling()
6. Deserialize each witness via witness_to_merkle_path()
7. Verify all merkle path roots are equal — TxBuildError::AnchorMismatch if not
8. Build zcash_primitives::transaction::builder::Builder with the verified anchor
9. add_sapling_spend for each note
10. add_sapling_output for the recipient amount
11. If change > 0: derive a change address (use sk.default_address().1), add_sapling_output for change
12. builder.build(...) with spend/output provers and ZIP-317 fee rule
13. Serialize transaction: tx.write(&mut bytes)
14. Return bytes

Defensive rules:
- Never log sk, note plaintext values, or recipient address — log only 'building tx: N inputs, amount=X zatoshi' at info level
- If builder.build() returns an error, wrap in TxBuildError::BuilderError(msg) — never unwrap
- If serialization fails: TxBuildError::BuilderError('tx serialization failed: ' + msg)
- amount_zatoshi == 0 is treated as dust (below DUST_THRESHOLD)
- Implement as a pure function (no async, no DB calls) — testable with synthetic data

Spawn at minimum three subagents in parallel during implementation:
- Subagent A: research zcash_primitives Builder::new signature and BuildConfig construction
- Subagent B: research how the ZIP-317 fee rule is passed to builder.build()
- Subagent C: research how the FVK and ExtendedSpendingKey are provided to the builder
acceptance_criteria: build_shielded_tx returns non-empty bytes for a synthetic 1-input 1-output tx; all TxBuildError variants are exercised in tests; no unwrap() in production code
close_reason: build_shielded_tx implemented in wallet/src/tx_builder.rs: dust check, address parsing, 2-pass fee-aware coin selection, note/witness reconstruction, anchor verification, sapling builder API, transaction serialization
